title: Understanding Reference Frames
text: To find the velocity of the particle in reference frame C, we need to consider the velocities of the particle and the reference frames relative to each other.

title: Velocity Addition
text: The velocity of the particle in reference frame C can be found by adding the velocity of the particle in reference frame A to the velocities of the reference frames relative to each other.

title: Calculate Velocity in Frame B
text: First, find the velocity of the particle in reference frame B. Use the velocity of the particle in reference frame A and the velocity of frame A with respect to frame B.

title: Calculate Velocity in Frame C
text: Now, find the velocity of the particle in reference frame C by adding the velocity of frame B with respect to frame C.

title: Final Velocity
text: The final velocity of the particle in reference frame C is the sum of the velocities calculated in the previous steps.
